name: AVL.Dict

aliases.name: Comparator
aliases.comment:
A comparator is a function which compares two keys.
So a `Dict ID User` is a dictionary
that lets you look up a `ID` (such as user ids)
and find the associated `User`.

    import AVL.Dict as Dict exposing (Comparator, Dict)

    type ID
        = ID Int

    idComparator : Comparator ID
    idComparator (ID x) (ID y) =
        compare x y

    users : Dict ID User
    users =
        Dict.fromListWith idComparator
            [ ( "Alice", User (ID 0) "Alice" 28 1.65 )
            , ( "Bob", User (ID 1) "Bob" 19 1.82 )
            , ( "Chuck", User (ID 2) "Chuck" 33 1.75 )
            ]

    alice : Maybe User
    alice =
        Dict.get (ID 0) users

    type alias User =
        { id : ID
        , name : String
        , age : Int
        , height : Float
        }

aliases.name: Dict
aliases.comment:
A dictionary of keys and values.
So a `Dict String User` is a dictionary
that lets you look up a `String` (such as user names)
and find the associated `User`.

    import AVL.Dict as Dict exposing (Dict)

    users : Dict String User
    users =
        Dict.fromList
            [ ( "Alice", User "Alice" 28 1.65 )
            , ( "Bob", User "Bob" 19 1.82 )
            , ( "Chuck", User "Chuck" 33 1.75 )
            ]

    type alias User =
        { name : String
        , age : Int
        , height : Float
        }

values.name: clear
values.comment:
Remove all entries from a dictionary.
Useful when you need to create new empty dictionary using same comparator.
values.type: AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value

values.name: diff
values.comment: Keep a key-value pair when its key does not appear in the right dictionary.
values.type: AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value

values.name: empty
values.comment: Create an empty dictionary.
values.type: AVL.Dict.Dict comparable value

values.name: emptyWith
values.comment: Create an empty dictionary with custom key comparator.
values.type: AVL.Dict.Comparator key -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value

values.name: filter
values.comment: Keep only the key-value pairs that pass the given test.
values.type: (key -> value -> Basics.Bool) -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value

values.name: foldl
values.comment: Fold over the key-value pairs in a dictionary from lowest key to highest key.
values.type: (key -> value -> acc -> acc) -> acc -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> acc

values.name: foldr
values.comment: Fold over the key-value pairs in a dictionary from highest key to lowest key.
values.type: (key -> value -> acc -> acc) -> acc -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> acc

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Convert an association list into a dictionary.
values.type: List.List ( comparable, value ) -> AVL.Dict.Dict comparable value

values.name: fromListWith
values.comment: Convert an association list into a dictionary with custom key comparator.
values.type: AVL.Dict.Comparator key -> List.List ( key, value ) -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value

values.name: get
values.comment:
Get the value associated with a key. If the key is not found, return Nothing.
This is useful when you are not sure if a key will be in the dictionary.

    animals =
        fromList [ ( "Tom", Cat ), ( "Jerry", Mouse ) ]

    get "Tom" animals == Just Cat
    get "Jerry" animals == Just Mouse
    get "Spike" animals == Nothing
values.type: key -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> Maybe.Maybe value

values.name: insert
values.comment:
Insert a key-value pair into a dictionary.
Replaces value when there is a collision.
values.type: key -> value -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value

values.name: intersect
values.comment:
Keep a key-value pair when its key appears in the right dictionary.
Preference is given to values in the left dictionary.
values.type: AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment: Determine if a dictionary is empty.
values.type: AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> Basics.Bool

values.name: keys
values.comment:
Get all of the keys in a dictionary, sorted from lowest to highest.

    keys (fromList [ ( 1, "Bob" ), ( 0, "Alice" ) ]) == [ 0, 1 ]
values.type: AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> List.List key

values.name: map
values.comment: Apply a function to all values in a dictionary.
values.type: (key -> a -> b) -> AVL.Dict.Dict key a -> AVL.Dict.Dict key b

values.name: member
values.comment: Determine if a key is in a dictionary.
values.type: key -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> Basics.Bool

values.name: merge
values.comment:
The most general way of combining two dictionaries.
You provide three accumulators for when a given key appears:

1.  Only in the left dictionary.
2.  In both dictionaries.
3.  Only in the right dictionary.

You then traverse all the keys from lowest to highest, building up whatever you want.
values.type: (key -> left -> acc -> acc) -> (key -> left -> right -> acc -> acc) -> (key -> right -> acc -> acc) -> AVL.Dict.Dict key left -> AVL.Dict.Dict key right -> acc -> acc

values.name: partition
values.comment:
Partition a dictionary according to some test.
The first dictionary contains all key-value pairs which passed the test,
and the second contains the pairs that did not.
values.type: (key -> value -> Basics.Bool) -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> ( AVL.Dict.Dict key value, AVL.Dict.Dict key value )

values.name: remove
values.comment:
Remove a key-value pair from a dictionary.
If the key is not found, no changes are made.
values.type: key -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Create a dictionary with one key-value pair.
values.type: comparable -> value -> AVL.Dict.Dict comparable value

values.name: singletonWith
values.comment: Create a dictionary with one key-value pair with custom key comparator.
values.type: AVL.Dict.Comparator key -> key -> value -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value

values.name: size
values.comment:
Determine the number of key-value pairs in the dictionary.
It takes constant time to request the size.
values.type: AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> Basics.Int

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Convert a dictionary into an association list of key-value pairs, sorted by keys.

    toList (fromList [ ( 1, "Bob" ), ( 0, "Alice" ) ]) == [ ( 0, "Alice" ), ( 1, "Bob" ) ]
values.type: AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> List.List ( key, value )

values.name: union
values.comment:
Combine two dictionaries.
If there is a collision, preference is given to the left dictionary.
values.type: AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value

values.name: update
values.comment: Update the value of a dictionary for a specific key with a given function.
values.type: key -> (Maybe.Maybe value -> Maybe.Maybe value) -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> AVL.Dict.Dict key value

values.name: values
values.comment:
Get all of the values in a dictionary, in the order of their keys.

    values (fromList [ ( 1, "Bob" ), ( 0, "Alice" ) ]) == [ "Alice", "Bob" ]
values.type: AVL.Dict.Dict key value -> List.List value
